page: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and EBS optimization. Network connectivity is a key feature, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m6in.metal demonstrates top-tier multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU tests and Passmark CPU scores. Its memory architecture delivers strong results, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 339.05 GB/sec, which is in the top 10% tier. Cryptographic capabilities are also notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated instructions like AES and VAES. Single-core performance, however, remains in the average tier, as shown by stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core results.

Given its high core density, massive memory bandwidth, and 200 Gbps network capacity, the m6in.metal is highly efficient for network-intensive, multi-threaded workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and software compilation. The absence of local storage means workloads must be designed around network-attached storage, making it ideal for distributed systems, enterprise applications, and memory-intensive data processing that can leverage the high-speed network interface.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. It includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPUs. The server is optimized for high-speed networking, providing a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. In benchmarks, the m6in.metal excels in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and uncached memory reads. It is highly suited for network-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale database operations, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps networking for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
Equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with no local storage
Delivers 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput applications
Achieves top-tier multi-core CPU and uncached memory bandwidth benchmark scores
Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and network-intensive workloads
